string.text: Bandit heroes will train their militias.
Chance each day that they might train further.
'Hardest' grants enough XP to significantly upgrade troops. 'Off' grants no bonus XP.
Chance per day that the militia will gain more troops (0 for off).
Grow each troop type by this percent.
They won't be attacked by Bandit Militias.
New Bandit Militias will form spontaneously as well as by merging together normally.
Bandit Militias will spawn hourly at this likelihood.
Bandit Militias won't merge or split a second time until this many hours go by.
Bandit Militias will move at normal speed regardless of their party size.
Militias defeated with fewer than this many remaining troops will be dispersed.
No Bandit Militias smaller than this will form.
Small looter and bandit parties won't merge.
How likely a Bandit Militia is to split when large enough.
Limit the per-piece value of equipment given to the Heroes. Mostly for when other mods give you Hero loot.
How many looters get made into better units when training.
Upgrade (at most) this percentage of troops when training occurs.
Major setting. Setting higher means more, bigger Bandit Militias.
Bandit Militias won't train any units past this tier.
10 means any party 10% weaker will be ignored. 100 attacks without restriction.
The chance of Bandit Militias AI considering raiding a village. Triggers once per in-game hour for every party, so a smaller value is advised.
What to name a Bandit Militia.
What to name a Bandit Militia with no leader.
Have omniscient view of Bandit Militias.
Any smaller Bandit Militias won't be tracked.
Bandit Militias will have unique banners, or use basic bandit clan ones.
When your fiefs are raided you'll see a banner message.
Remove the messages of Bandit Militia Heroes being taken or released as prisoners.
Double-check if the bandit voice lines match the gender. Some official lines don't specify gender, so female leaders might use male voices if disabled.
Chance for Bandit Militia leaders to be female. 0 is all male, 100 is all female.
The log file is at 'Documents/Mount and Blade II Bannerlord/Configs/ModLogs/BanditMilitia_[Time].log'.
Enables debug logging and hourly spawns. (Teleport removed)
Enable features related to Warsails DLC (e.g. Sea Raiders).
Allow Sea Raiders to form militias.
{SETTLEMENT} has been raided! {PARTY} is fleeing near {TOWN} with the loot!
What do you want? We're not playing games here.
Fight me if you have the guts!
We are the free people of Calradia. We take what we need.
Take these {BRIBE_COST} denars. Leave us alone.
You look like skilled warriors. Join me.
A tempting offer, warlord. We are with you!
We serve only ourselves. Prepare to die!
Halt! This is Warlord territory. Pay the toll or bleed.
What do you want, traveler? We are on patrol.
Here is {GOLD_COST} denars. Let us pass.
A wise choice. You may pass.
You look capable. Join my army and grow rich.
Better than the Warlord's offer. We are with you!
I challenge your leader to a duel! (1v1)
Hah! You have guts. Let's dance!
I challenge your leader to a duel! Winner takes all.
Bold words. Let's see if your steel is as sharp as your tongue! (Duel Starting...)
Argh... You are strong... Take the loot and go.
Hah! Another weakling. Get out before I finish you.
You defeated the Warlord in single combat! The bandits are awestruck.
The Warlord disarmed you and kicked you into the dirt. You are dragged away in chains.
[Tactical Brilliance] {RANK} {NAME} is fielding units specifically countered against your army composition!
You managed to escape during the confusion of the battle!
I am here to collect protection money. Pay up.
We don't want trouble... take this and leave us be.
We have nothing for you, vulture! Get out!
[Extortion] The villagers refuse! 'Come and take it if you dare!'
[Bandit Militias] The militia has no cells for prisoners. They strip you of {GOLD} denars and leave you for dead.
{PLAYER}... You lost one? You'll pay for this.
You killed one of my men. I'm counting, {PLAYER}.
That fighter fell. My rage grows, {PLAYER}.
Three of my militia? THREE! {PLAYER} — I will find you.
The third fell too. Now it's personal, {PLAYER}.
You killed ten of my men. All forces: {PLAYER} must die.
THE TENTH. {PLAYER} — I wrote your name in blood. It stays there.
[BanditMilitias] CRITICAL ERROR: Hideout list failed! No hideouts found.
[BanditMilitias] CRITICAL: Globals could not be initialized!
[BanditMilitias] SPAWN ABORTED: '{HIDE_NAME}' coordinates are invalid. Will retry.
[BanditMilitias] Campaign behaviors could not be registered. Mod will run in limited mode.
Master switch for militia spawning. Disabled = hideouts behave like vanilla.
Hard cap on active militia parties across the entire map.
Days to wait after a militia is destroyed before a new one spawns from the same hideout.
Days after the world map opens before spawning begins. Applies to both Campaign and Sandbox.
Allows Sea Raider hideouts to spawn militias.
Militias receive unique, flavourful names instead of the generic 'Bandit Militia'.
Bandits can establish brand-new hideouts in lawless regions.
Number of nearby bandit parties required to trigger a new hideout.
Minimum days between hideout formation attempts.
Formation is blocked when lord patrol density exceeds this value. Higher = easier formation.
Daily chance for an abandoned hideout to attract nearby bandit parties back to it.
Powerful 'Anti-Hero' warlords emerge to command bandit forces. Disabling this reduces the mod to a simple spawn enhancer.
Advanced militia AI: territory control, swarm coordination, strategic retreats. Disabled = vanilla AI logic.
Warlords and their bodyguards slowly regenerate HP during battle.
Warlords analyze the player's army composition and field counter-units (cavalry vs. infantry vs. ranged).
Regional bandit strength required before a Warlord can emerge.
Minimum days a Captain must survive before being eligible for Warlord promotion.
Minimum battles a Captain must win before Warlord promotion.
If no Warlord exists after this many days, the top 3 Captains are force-promoted.
Maximum concurrent Warlords on the map. (Recommended: 5 | Chaos: 20+)
If Minor Lords exceed the cap, excess ones are weakened to Warlord level — making them easier to eliminate.
Militias study the player's combat style (cavalry / ranged / infantry / mixed) and adapt counter-strategies.
How quickly doctrine confidence shifts after battle outcomes. Higher = faster adaptation.
Minimum hours before a Warlord can switch to a different doctrine.
Fine-tune doctrine aggression. Negative = cautious, Positive = reckless.
Enables alliances, rivalries, and betrayals between active warlords.
Warlords can run propaganda campaigns to erode town loyalty.
Warlords bribe officials to establish underground networks, earning daily gold income.
Gold cost per militia member during diplomacy bribe events.
Minimum relation score required for two warlords to form an alliance.
Relations at or below this value trigger open rivalry.
An allied warlord may betray the other once relations fall below this value.
How quickly political relations drift back toward neutrality each day.
Upgrades bandits with better gear and combat stats. Highly recommended for mid-to-late game.
Flat skill point bonus applied to enhanced bandits.
Overall gear quality for enhanced bandits. 0 = vanilla, 5 = elite.
Defeating stronger enemies can reward Focus points or rare high-tier items.
Multiplies gold dropped by enhanced bandits.
Multiplies Renown earned from mod battles.
Additional loot multiplier exclusive to Warlord battles.
Reward multiplier for hard-fought battles. 2.0 = double rewards.
Delayed militia bounty reward paid per defeated troop on the next daily tick.
Small chance to gain an Attribute or Focus point from very challenging battles.
Chance to receive high-quality item drops from difficult battles.
Scales total bandit population (vanilla + mod). High values significantly impact performance. Requires restart.
Multiplies maximum party size for all bandit groups.
Increases spawn rates in kingdoms currently at war. Chaos breeds bandits!
Increases spawn rates near heavy caravan routes and prosperous settlements.
Flat AI aggression bonus during night hours.
Flat AI aggression bonus in forested terrain.
Strength ratio required before a militia will attack a lord's patrol. Lower = more aggressive.
Villages pay tribute or betray warlords based on accumulated fear.
Fallen warlords leave fear echoes and inherited tactics that affect future spawns in the same hideout.
Dynamic crisis events triggered by warlord actions — raids, sieges, betrayals.
Warlords can rise through ranks: Outlaw -> Warlord -> Minor Lord -> Recognized Lord.
Notorious warlords attract bounties and dedicated hunter parties.
Militia parties can raid villages for loot and to spread fear.
Multiplies the gold earned from militia raids.
Captured warlords attempt to escape imprisonment via bribes or underground contacts.
Warlords manage supply lines and resource extraction to sustain their armies.
Minimum hours of captivity before escape attempts begin.
Hard cap on captivity length. Player is released when exceeded.
Base hourly escape chance after the lock period.
Additional hourly escape chance per Roguery skill point.
Upper cap for hourly escape chance.
Days after lock ends to reach full escape chance.
Enables a lightweight FeedForward neural network that advises the heuristic AI. It learns from battle outcomes over time.
Logs battle samples to CSV for telemetry and potential training.
Records neural features without updating weights. Useful for establishing baselines.
Neural weight reward multiplier when militia beats an enemy 1.5x its size.
Neural weight reward multiplier when militia beats an enemy 2.0x its size.
Neural weight reward multiplier when militia beats an enemy 3.0x its size.
Sampling interval for battle data logging. 1 = log every battle.
Automatically optimizes neural weights to maintain focus on relevant features.
Maximum number of recent battle experiences stored for on-the-fly learning.
Writes detailed diagnostics to the game log. For debugging only — expect log spam.
DANGER: Removes ALL mod parties for safe uninstallation. THIS CANNOT BE UNDONE.
Removes idle or weak parties during cleanup passes. Enable only if experiencing lag.
Writes all mod events to logs/BanditMilitias.log.
Verbose logging of AI reasoning and decision outcomes.
Displays [DEBUG] popups in-game for ongoing mod activity.
Spreads AI processing across ticks to avoid frame spikes.
CPU budget for the AI Scheduler. Lower = smoother FPS, slower AI updates.
Experimental: offloads non-critical AI tasks to background threads. May cause rare race conditions.
AI factors in territory control, battle history, and proximity when making decisions.
Troops recruited by AI per reinforcement cycle.
XP accumulated before a militia can upgrade its troops.
Troop count at which AI considers a party 'strong' for strategic planning.
Gold amount at which AI considers itself 'wealthy' and pursues aggressive expansion.
I'm just a villager, I don't know anything.
